codeReview.summary: The feature wires logprobs into the legacy /v1/completions endpoint across three files. The non-streaming path correctly uses the OpenAI legacy completions logprobs format (tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset). However, the streaming path in SSEEncoder.textCompletionStream uses the chat completions logprobs format (logprobs.content array of {token, logprob, top_logprobs} objects) instead of the legacy format, creating a structural inconsistency that will break clients expecting the legacy completions format. The logprobs type coercion (int/bool/double) for lm-eval compatibility is correct. Zero-overhead guarantee for normal (non-logprobs) completions is preserved.

issues.description: Streaming logprobs format mismatch: textCompletionStream uses the chat completions format with `logprobs.content` (array of {token, logprob, top_logprobs} objects) instead of the legacy completions format (tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset as separate arrays). The non-streaming path in OpenAIRoutes.swift correctly uses the legacy format, creating an inconsistency. Per architecture.md invariants: 'Legacy format exactness: tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset must match OpenAI legacy shape exactly for lm-eval parse_logprobs().' The streaming chunks should use the same nested array format as non-streaming, with per-chunk offsets. Example correct streaming format: {"logprobs": {"tokens": [" t"], "token_logprobs": [-2.1], "top_logprobs": [{" t": -2.1}], "text_offset": [0]}} instead of {"logprobs": {"content": [{"token": " t", "logprob": -2.1, "top_logprobs": [...]}]}}

text_offset uses lp.token.utf8.count (byte count) instead of character count. OpenAI's documentation describes text_offset as character offsets. For ASCII tokens this is equivalent, but for tokens containing multi-byte Unicode characters (CJK, emoji), byte offsets and character offsets diverge. Low severity because lm-eval evaluation datasets are primarily ASCII-focused, but technically non-compliant with the spec.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The streaming vs non-streaming logprobs format for the /v1/completions endpoint uses two different wire formats. The non-streaming path uses the legacy format (separate arrays for tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset), while the streaming path uses the chat completions format (content array of per-token objects). AGENTS.md and architecture.md specify 'Legacy format exactness' but only document the non-streaming format, not the streaming format. This gap allowed the inconsistent streaming implementation to pass static analysis.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md invariants section only describes the legacy format as 'tokens, token_logprobs, top_logprobs, text_offset' without specifying that BOTH streaming and non-streaming paths must use this format. SSEEncoder.swift line ~240 uses content array format for textCompletionStream.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The logprobs type coercion pattern (handling Bool, Int, Double for the logprobs field) is only implemented in the /v1/completions route handler. If future endpoints need the same coercion, this logic should be extracted into a shared utility.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: OpenAIRoutes.swift lines 317-323: inline closure for logprobsValue coercion. This is the only place that handles logprobs: 1 (integer) serde.

summary: FAIL — One blocking issue: the streaming SSE path in textCompletionStream uses the chat completions logprobs format (logprobs.content array) instead of the legacy completions format (separate tokens/token_logprobs/top_logprobs/text_offset arrays), creating a format inconsistency with the non-streaming path which correctly uses the legacy format. The feature's stated requirement is 'Emit logprobs in textCompletionStream SSE' and the architecture invariant states 'Legacy format exactness' for lm-eval compatibility. The streaming format must be converted to match the non-streaming legacy format. One non-blocking issue: text_offset uses UTF-8 byte count instead of character count. Positive findings: logprobs type coercion (int/bool/double) is correct, zero-overhead guarantee for normal completions is preserved, the non-streaming path format is correct, and ChatRequest parameter plumbing is clean.
